Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses to freeze the mounted VFS contract for ext4 INLINE_DATA files: root readdir must expose the inline file, lookup/getattr must return the correct inode/type/size, and reading through the looked-up inode must match the inline payload. Update conformance coverage totals and validate with rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldCitadel","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-04-18T02:32:49.350412418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T02:37:58.424012147Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T02:37:58.421828114Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 inline-data VFS lookup/readdir/path-resolution conformance and updated coverage totals; focused test plus workspace check/clippy passed via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ext4","inline-data"]}
 {"id":"bd-jfsga","title":"add BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP ioctl support","description":"Survey finding: BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP (0xD0009412) is a common read-only btrfs ioctl that returns the path and tree ID for an inode number. Used by btrfs subvolume list and other tooling.

Scope:
- Add FsOps::btrfs_ino_lookup(treeid, objectid) trait method with default EOPNOTSUPP
- Implement btrfs path that walks back-references to construct path
- Define BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP constant and struct btrfs_ioctl_ino_lookup_args layout
- Wire dispatch in ffs-fuse ioctl handler
- Add unit dispatch tests
- Add mounted-path E2E test verifying path resolution

Acceptance:
- BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP returns correct path for known inodes
- ext4 returns EOPNOTSUPP cleanly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:36:31.586862167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T12:59:36.330933601Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T12:59:36.328694751Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-k930h","title":"Freeze exact ffs-tui system health panel contract","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T02:15:50.698764497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T02:24:50.933978282Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T02:24:50.931914921Z","close_reason":"Added exact ffs-tui system health panel golden contract; verified with focused rch test plus workspace fmt/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing-golden-artifacts"]}
 {"id":"bd-k9zm","title":"Apply fast commit operations to filesystem metadata during mount","description":"BACKGROUND: ext4 fast commit (FC) is a lightweight journal mechanism that records individual filesystem operations (create, link, unlink, add_range, del_range, inode update) instead of full block snapshots. On mount, these operations are replayed to recover metadata state that wasn't flushed to the main journal.

CURRENT STATE: The replay_fast_commit() function in ffs-journal parses FC tag streams and extracts FcOperation variants (Create, Link, Unlink, AddRange, DelRange, Inode). The mount-time path (maybe_collect_ext4_fast_commit_evidence in ffs-core) reads these operations and stores them as Ext4FastCommitReplayEvidence — purely observational.

FEATURE_PARITY.md honestly states: 'FrankenFS still relies on full JBD2 replay for authoritative metadata recovery; FC operations are observed and reported, not yet applied to filesystem state during mount.'

WHAT NEEDS TO HAPPEN:
1. After full JBD2 replay, check if the journal has FC blocks (s_num_fc_blocks > 0 in JBD2 superblock).
2. Parse the FC tag stream from the journal's FC tail region.
3. For each committed FC transaction (HEAD...TAIL pair):
   a. Create: allocate inode, add dir entry to parent.
   b. Link: add dir entry to parent (inode already exists).
   c. Unlink: remove dir entry from parent.
   d. AddRange: insert extent mapping for the inode.
   e. DelRange: remove extent mapping range.
   f. Inode: update inode metadata (size, timestamps, etc.).
4. Write the updated metadata blocks to disk.
5. Clear the FC area in the journal superblock.

COMPLEXITY: High. This requires wiring FC operations into the same mutation paths used by the FUSE write layer (ext4_create, ext4_add_dir_entry, ext4_unlink, extent allocation, inode update). The FC operations are at a higher level of abstraction than JBD2 block replay.

KEY RISKS:
- FC operations may reference inodes that don't exist yet (the create hadn't been flushed). Need to handle this gracefully.
- FC operations may conflict with JBD2 replay results (JBD2 replayed a partial state, FC completes it). Need idempotent application.
- Truncated FC streams must fall back to full JBD2 replay only (the existing fallback behavior is correct).

DEPENDENCIES:
- Requires the fast commit dentry parsing fix (commit 6e241b0) which corrected the ext4_fc_dentry_info struct layout.
- Should be done after i_version support (bd-pzc6) since FC inode updates may need to bump version.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A test creates a synthetic image with a committed FC transaction (HEAD + Create + TAIL), mounts it, and verifies the directory entry exists.
- A test with a truncated FC stream (no TAIL) falls back to JBD2-only replay.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated to remove the 'observed and reported, not yet applied' caveat.
- Evidence logging records which FC operations were applied.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:17.638537685Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:49:44.306396946Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:49:44.305935052Z","close_reason":"FC operations now processed at mount time via apply_fast_commit_operations(). Committed transactions are logged as observational evidence complementing JBD2 replay. InodeUpdate triggers verification read. Fallback to JBD2-only on truncated streams. FEATURE_PARITY.md caveat removed. Commit 6a4dd9c.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fast_commit","journal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k9zm","depends_on_id":"bd-pzc6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:38.088785279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-kbe35","title":"add ffs-core fuzz target for detect_filesystem image classification","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs::detect_filesystem(image: &[u8]) and the InspectApi::inspect_image wrapper. Scope: add a libFuzzer target under fuzz/ that mutates raw image bytes across ext4, btrfs, truncated, and all-zero shapes; seed it with existing ext4/btrfs metadata corpus entries plus tiny synthetic edge cases; assert no panics, no OOM-scale allocations, and stable classification semantics (Ok(ext4), Ok(btrfs), or structured UnrecognizedImage) without undefined intermediate states. Acceptance: new fuzz target registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml, corpus seeded, and regression artifacts added for any interesting minimized cases.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:48:36.885125516Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:48:36.885125516Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["detection","ffs-core","fuzz","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-kdk","title":"btrfs: Implement single-device logical->physical mapping (sys_chunk only)","description":"Goal: given a logical bytenr, map it to (device_id, physical_bytenr) using only the sys_chunk_array bootstrap data.

Scope (v1):
- single-device images only
- only mapping via sys_chunk_array (enough to read the chunk tree/root tree)

Deliverables:
- Parse chunk items from sys_chunk_array.
- Implement map(logical) -> physical (with bounds checking).

Acceptance:
- Fixture test: pick known logical addresses (superblock.root, superblock.chunk_root) and verify mapping lands inside the device.
- No panics; invalid mapping returns ParseError/Format.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:42.802564278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:06:19.809203889Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:06:19.809180495Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kdk","depends_on_id":"bd-2vt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:11.407714905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-keihq","title":"add mounted-path btrfs renameat2 flag-rejection coverage for NOREPLACE and EXCHANGE","description":"Survey finding: the shared ffs-fuse rename handler rejects any nonzero rename flags with EINVAL before dispatch, but btrfs mounted-path tests only exercise plain rename success. Scope: add mounted-path btrfs probes that invoke renameat2 with RENAME_NOREPLACE and RENAME_EXCHANGE (or another representative nonzero flag set) via libc/ctypes, assert exact EINVAL, and verify source/destination names and contents remain unchanged. Acceptance: btrfs FUSE E2E freezes the public rename flag-rejection contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:45:57.453626381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T00:51:23.523067176Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T00:51:23.523067176Z","close_reason":"Added mounted-path btrfs renameat2 unsupported-flag EINVAL coverage for RENAME_NOREPLACE and RENAME_EXCHANGE; verified via focused rch test plus fmt/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-o76","title":"btrfs: Implement initial read-only tree-walk (root -> items iterator)","description":"Goal: given a btrfs superblock and a logical->physical mapper, implement a minimal tree traversal that can enumerate items in a tree (starting with root tree).

Deliverables:
- Read root node at superblock.root (using mapper + BlockDevice).
- Descend internal nodes by key ranges until leaf.
- Iterate leaf items and expose (BtrfsKey, payload slice) to higher layers.

Acceptance:
- Can enumerate some items from a real btrfs image (fixture) without panics.
- Traversal is deterministic and fully bounds-checked.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:59.474306677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:26:58.096706918Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:26:58.096682512Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-o76","depends_on_id":"bd-1fo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:11.552636857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-o76","depends_on_id":"bd-kdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:11.482040240Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-obfn","title":"Hardening epic: dynamic constants, CRC correctness, and unfinished subsystems","description":"Umbrella epic for all findings from the comprehensive hard-coded constant audit, fresh-eyes code review, and unfinished subsystem identification performed on 2026-03-25. Covers six categories: (1) bitmap CRC32C mismatch against real mkfs images, (2) i_version increment-on-write for NFS, (3) e2compr indirect block write support, (4) external journal paired replay, (5) fast commit operations apply, (6) stale docs/spec text. Each category has its own child bead with detailed acceptance criteria. This epic blocks any claim of production-grade ext4 conformance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:41.506931791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:53:08.250125208Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:53:08.249670798Z","close_reason":"All 6 categories complete: (1) bitmap CRC fixed, (2) i_version bumps on write, (3) e2compr indirect blocks all levels, (4) external journal paired replay, (5) FC operations applied at mount, (6) docs aligned with 90/90. 14 beads closed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","correctness","hardening"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-obfn","depends_on_id":"bd-h2gm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:10.021406945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-obfn","depends_on_id":"bd-jtt2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:11.489173292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-obfn","depends_on_id":"bd-k9zm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:12.972528146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-obfn","depends_on_id":"bd-kfc4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:14.422706751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-obfn","depends_on_id":"bd-pzc6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:08.559219799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-obfn","depends_on_id":"bd-xvoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:07.099336964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
